title: Crypto Tracker – Top 150 Coins with Live Filters & 12-Month Price Projections

requirements.text: Replace the existing multi-crypto dashboard with a new full-page dashboard that displays the top 150 cryptocurrencies ranked by market cap. Fetch live data from the CoinGecko public API (markets endpoint, vs_currency=usd, per_page=150, page=1). Display each coin in a sortable, paginated table with columns: Rank, Name/Symbol, Price, 24h Change, Market Cap, 24h Volume, Buy Ratio (approximated as 24h volume / market cap × 100, shown as a percentage), and Fully Diluted Market Cap.

quotes: tracks the top 150 cryptocurrencies by market cap
live market cap, volume, buy ratio, and fully diluted market cap

acceptanceCriteria: Table renders 150 rows of live CoinGecko data on load
Each row shows: rank, coin name, symbol, logo, current price, 24h % change (color-coded green/red), market cap, 24h volume, buy ratio %, and fully diluted market cap
Table is sortable by clicking any column header
Table supports pagination or virtual scrolling for performance
Data auto-refreshes every 60 seconds

requirements.text: Add a filter/control bar above the top-150 table with dropdown selectors that allow the user to filter or sort the list by each of the following metrics individually: Live Market Cap (high to low / low to high), 24h Volume (high to low / low to high), Buy Ratio (high to low / low to high), and Fully Diluted Market Cap (high to low / low to high). Include a search input to filter coins by name or symbol. Include a reset button to clear all active filters.

quotes: specific dropdown tools to filter each cryptocurrency to track live market cap, volume, buy ratio, and fully diluted market cap

acceptanceCriteria: Four dropdown selectors exist: one each for Market Cap, Volume, Buy Ratio, and Fully Diluted Market Cap
Selecting a dropdown option re-sorts or filters the table immediately without page reload
Search input filters the visible rows by name or ticker symbol in real time
Reset button clears all dropdowns and search input and restores default market-cap ranking
Active filter state is visually indicated on the dropdown buttons

requirements.text: Add a dedicated 'Price Projections' section below the main table. This section contains a coin selector dropdown (searchable, showing all 150 coins) and, once a coin is selected, displays a 12-month rolling price projection panel. The projection must show: a monthly price target table (Month 1 through Month 12 from today), a projected price range (low / mid / high) per month, a confidence score per month, and a line chart visualising the projected price trajectory over the 12-month period. Projections are algorithmically derived on the frontend using the coin's current price, 30-day historical trend from CoinGecko, and simple momentum/regression logic — no external AI API call required.

quotes: separate section to filter each cryptocurrencies price projections on a current, 12-month running schedule

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Price Projections' section is rendered below the main tracker table
A searchable dropdown lets the user pick any of the 150 tracked coins
After selecting a coin, a 12-row monthly projection table appears (Month 1 – Month 12)
Each row shows projected low, mid, and high price plus a confidence percentage
A Recharts line chart visualises the mid-price projection curve with shaded bands for low/high range
Projections update when the selected coin changes
The projection methodology is documented with a short disclaimer note in the UI

requirements.text: Implement a CoinGecko data-fetching layer using React Query with in-memory caching and rate-limit-safe request batching. Use the existing useQueries.ts pattern. Add a new hook (e.g. useTopCoins) that fetches the top 150 coins market data and a hook (e.g. useCoinHistory) that fetches 30-day historical price data for a selected coin. Both hooks must handle loading, error, and empty states gracefully and fall back to cached data on API errors.

quotes: tracks the top 150 cryptocurrencies by market cap

acceptanceCriteria: useTopCoins hook returns 150 coins with all required fields
useCoinHistory hook accepts a coinId and returns 30 data points
React Query cache is used — repeated renders do not fire duplicate requests within the cache window
Loading skeletons are shown while data is fetching
Error boundary or error message is shown if the API fails

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive dark-mode-first visual theme to the entire application. Use a dark charcoal/near-black base with neon cyan and gold accent colors. Use a clean sans-serif typeface. The header should display 'Crypto Tracker AI' branding. Cards, tables, and dropdowns should use subtle glassmorphism or frosted-panel styling. Positive price changes must render in emerald green, negative in red. The layout must be fully responsive down to 375 px mobile width.
acceptanceCriteria: App renders in dark mode by default with charcoal backgrounds and neon cyan / gold accents
All table rows, cards, and dropdowns use consistent glassmorphism styling
Positive 24h change values are green, negative values are red
Layout is fully usable on a 375 px wide mobile screen
Header shows 'Crypto Tracker AI' title

constraints: Use only the CoinGecko public API (no API key required) for all live market data
All price projection logic must run client-side — no backend AI or external projection API calls
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Keep all Motoko backend logic in backend/main.mo (no new backend files unless migration is needed)
nonGoals: User authentication or Internet Identity login for this feature
Backend storage of market data or projections
News feed or social sentiment integration

required: Dark futuristic hero banner with abstract cryptocurrency network nodes and glowing cyan lines on a near-black background (crypto-hero-banner.dim_1440x320.png)
